page: Amazon Web Services m8id.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance across memory and processing workloads. The instance achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth tests, including read-only small block cached operations at 7680.87 GB/sec and uncached large block reads at 385.12 GB/sec. Multi-core CPU performance is strong, with a Passmark CPU score of 107119.74 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 658.26 GB/sec. The processor's support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) contributes to top-tier prompt processing speeds in LLM inference benchmarks, though text generation speeds remain in the average to strong range.

From a resource density perspective, the inclusion of 5.7 TB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside high-speed DDR5 memory provides a balanced hardware profile that eliminates the need for external block storage for high-throughput tasks. This makes the instance highly cost-efficient for data-intensive applications. The combination of high memory bandwidth, dedicated CPU allocation, and fast local storage makes it well-suited for database operations, Redis deployments, static web serving, and software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, database operations, and multi-core CPU tasks, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. The instance offers strong qualitative cost efficiency by bundling high-capacity local NVMe storage with dense DDR5 memory, reducing reliance on external storage resources. It is highly suited for high-throughput database workloads, Redis caching layers, software compilation, and large-scale web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5.7 TB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.24xlarge features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5.7 TB local NVMe storage for high-performance workloads.
tagline: A high-density general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, fast DDR5 memory, and massive local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 3.9 GHz
384 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with top-tier bandwidth
5700 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD instance storage
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in database operations and Redis caching
Hardware-accelerated vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX)
